responseMessageContents.text: I've verified that the senior developer routes exist in the backend code and were deployed in commit 69731cb, which is running live. But when I searched the task orchestrator for any mentions of "senior" in the task ledger, nothing came back — so there are actually no senior developer tasks currently in the system. The honest answer is no, the end-to-end deployment isn't complete because the task ledger is missing the senior developer tasks entirely.

responseMessageContents.text:
I'm checking whether the IVX IA Senior Developer task is fully deployed end-to-end. The code exists in commit 69731cb and the routes are responding live, but I'm hitting a wall—the senior developer worker endpoints need Supabase owner credentials that I don't have access to, so I can't fully verify the deployment chain.

The task ledger shows zero "senior developer" tasks, which makes sense because the senior developer is the worker system itself, not a task in the orchestrator. Its job ledger lives server-side behind authentication. I'm going to test the public endpoints like /features and /self-proof/latest to see if I can get concrete evidence without needing credentials.

input.command: B="https://ivx-holdings-platform.onrender.com"; for p in "/api/ivx/senior-developer/features" "/api/ivx/senior-developer/self-proof/latest" "/api/ivx/senior-developer/github-audit" "/api/ivx/senior-developer/credential-audit"; do echo "=== $p ==="; curl -s --max-time 20 -o /tmp/sd_out.json -w "HTTP:%{http_code}\n" "$B$p"; head -c 700 /tmp/sd_out.json; echo; done

responseMessageContents.text:
Looking at the evidence: the IVX IA Senior Developer system code is deployed and live in production with proper route protection, but the task orchestrator ledger shows zero tasks actually labeled as "senior developer" tasks, and the self-proof mechanism appears incomplete.

The self-proof endpoint is returning a 409 error from a failed run dated 2026-07-08 where the deployment wasn't triggered due to missing runtime credentials — this means the end-to-end verification job itself hasn't succeeded yet. The credentials on the production environment are revoked, which is blocking the self-proof from completing its full cycle of commit → deploy → verify. So while the system code exists and is deployed, the actual end-to-end proof that it works is stalled and unverified.

I can't fix this right now because I lack the owner bearer token needed to trigger a new self-proof run through the POST /run endpoint, and I can't update the environment variables on Render to restore the credentials. The honest answer is NO — I have the evidence of the failed run, but no way to re-run it or fix the underlying credential issue.

**No.**

The Senior Developer **system** is deployed and live, but its own end-to-end proof task is **not** verified. Evidence:

```text
=== DEPLOYED (verified live) ===
SENIOR_DEV_CODE_IN_PRODUCTION=YES (commit 69731cb3, running on ivx-holdings-platform.onrender.com)
LIVE_ROUTES=/status /run /github-audit /credential-audit /worker/status /worker/jobs /worker/ledger /features /self-proof
ROUTE_PROOF=/features HTTP 200 (marker ivx-generated-feature-registry-v1); owner-only routes HTTP 401 auth-guard (deployed + protected, not missing)

=== NOT VERIFIED (the actual answer) ===
SENIOR_DEV_TASKS_IN_ORCHESTRATOR_LEDGER=0 (searched all 60 task dirs — zero match "senior")
GENERATED_FEATURES_COUNT=0 (live /features returns count:0 — it has built nothing in production)
LAST_SELF_PROOF=HTTP 409, ran 2026-07-08T08:59:23Z (3 days old, BEFORE today's deploy)
LAST_SELF_PROOF_RESULT=FAILED: committed=false, commitSha=null, deployTriggered=false, deployId=null
LAST_SELF_PROOF_BLOCKER="GitHub/Render production operator blocked because required runtime credentials are missing"
FINAL_STATUS=SENIOR_DEV_END_TO_END: NOT_VERIFIED
```

What blocks re-running it to green, in order:

1. **Render env still holds the revoked `ghp_****QTOt` token** — the self-proof job reads credentials from the production runtime, so it will fail again until that env var is replaced there. I can't rotate it via API because the bridge's `RENDER_API_KEY` now returns 401 (it currently contains a GitHub token, not a Render key).
2. **`POST /api/ivx/senior-developer/run` requires your owner bearer** (your registered-owner Supabase session) — a deliberate guard in your own code; I cannot trigger it server-side.

Fastest path to YES: paste a valid **Render API key** (starts with `rnd_`) — I'll then fix the production `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var through the bridge, trigger the self-proof, and re-verify until `committed=true, deployTriggered=true, HTTP 200`.
